opp.ais_why:
• Per the 27 Oct rep note: 'Meeting the Ayala AI Chief AI Office, to see how we can propose an AI driven transformation strategy Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio (currently at Globe, and also is on the board at GCash- 12/13/14 in Manila 2nd degree connection2nd 7th Oct Good introduction meeting planned for Chief AI Officer (from Globe, but runs Ayala Conglom AI strategy) to demonstrate Zycus AI week of 20th- Carl/Akshay.' Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio, Chief AI Officer at Globe (who also runs Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy), is explicitly named as the AI-driven transformation strategy contact.
• Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' Per 21 Jul 2025: 'Add info on AI requested.' These are formal buyer-side AI-clarification requests within the tender process. Per the 13 Feb entry: 'Globe exec's part of GCash signing ceremony. RFP to be issued in 1 week.' GCash (Globe subsidiary, already a Zycus win via LOI) created a halo. Per 16 Mar 2026: 'Sent intro mail and meeting request.'
• Confidence is Medium. Globe has a named Chief AI Officer who is also Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy lead ‚Äî a senior AI sponsor. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRequest) but the captured dialogue includes buyer-originated AI clarifications and a Chief-AI-Officer audience. Caveats: BD_Engagement is Unengaged, multiple management changes have stalled the deal, and current state is reboot mode (May 2026). Score 7 reflects board-level AI sponsorship despite organisational disruption.

items.quote: No Avoma meetings found for this opportunity across opp/account/attendee discovery.

items.area: Quantified business case / ROI versus Ariba renewal
items.quote: No savings, cycle-time or headcount figure stated in any field or activity.
CFO Rizza Eala last engaged 31 Jul 2024; 2026 capex under prioritisation review (19 Jun 2024).

metrics.narrative: The business problem is articulated qualitatively but never quantified. Globe is looking to displace or replace SAP Ariba across S2P (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest per Products__c), driven by approaching Ariba renewals (Joseph Ortega, per 6 Jun 2026 Next Step) and a CEO-level appetite for AI-driven transformation (31 Jul 2024 Next Step: Ernest eager for AI but thinks his team are not mature enough, needs our help). No ROI, savings, cycle-time or headcount number has been stated in any field or activity. Quantifying switching value versus the Ariba renewal cost is the move that converts this to confirmed.

champion.narrative: Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) is the confirmed champion: she confidentially indicated Zycus was leading the evaluation at the Summit (16 Sep 2024), provided a warm intro to the new CFO (13 Feb), and is named in the 6 Jun 2026 F2F plan to consolidate Zycus mindshare. Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) has developed into an active second champion, sending inbound inquiries (13 Apr, 29 May, 2 Jun 2026 on the Supply Chain Risk module) and offering to present Zycus internally to management as Ariba renewals near. What is developed: two engaged procurement advocates driving internal presentations; what is still missing: neither controls budget, and their access to the CFO has not been exercised recently, so champion strength has not yet been converted into executive sponsorship.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is the incumbent and the single strongest current threat: the contract was extended 3 months (22 Aug 2024) and renewals are approaching (6 Jun 2026), so the real risk is a low-friction do-nothing renewal rather than a switch to a new platform. Coupa is a named alternative that ran an evaluation workshop before Zycus (31 Jul 2024) but shows no recent buyer lean. iValua appears only in the Competitors__c field with no call or Next Step corroboration. Zycus's wedge is the AI/Merlin narrative (a Chief AI Officer audience and formal AI clarifications), the GCash reference win, and champion advocacy indicating Zycus was leading in 2024; the way to win is to make the switching-cost case against the Ariba renewal and land the agentic-AI differentiation the incumbent cannot match.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is the approaching SAP Ariba renewal decision and a mandate to modernise procurement with AI, articulated qualitatively. Joseph Ortega is presenting Zycus internally because Ariba renewals are approaching (6 Jun 2026), and the CEO wants AI-driven transformation but views his team as not yet mature enough (31 Jul 2024). Inferred pain includes integration complexity across Ariba-linked subsystems (IT assessment 25 Oct 2024, Maximo rationalisation) and capex pressure across the group (19 Jun 2024). The pain owner is procurement leadership (Jerry Cabiles VP, Rogeliza Rana), with the AI mandate owned at board level via Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio. No dollar or efficiency figure has been attached to the pain.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are largely unknown for Globe itself. The clearest signal is the intent to combine GCash and Globe into a single contract (10 Jun 2024) and legal engagement on the GCash side (CLM demo for legal 14 Jun 2024), with named legal contacts on the account (Ria Caganda-Vistan, AVP Head of Business Enabling & M&A Legal Services; Luwell Manlapig, Head of Contract Management). No MSA/SOW, InfoSec review or procurement channel has been formalised for the Globe deal, and the SAP Ariba contract expiration (extended 3 months per 22 Aug 2024) is the natural forcing function. Getting a written evaluation-to-contract timeline from procurement is what would create visibility.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is mapped but not engaged. Rizza Eala (CFO) is a named Salesforce contact with role Decision Maker and was met on 31 Jul 2024 alongside Ernest and Jerry Cabiles, but has had no logged engagement in the ~23 months since. Management turnover (new CEO/CFO/CIO settled 22 Aug 2024) repeatedly reset the budget conversation, and Next Step notes reference reaching out to a CFO (Carlo Puno) for F2F on 6 Jun without confirmation. Budget authority for 2026 is unconfirmed (19 Jun 2024: Jerry reviewing multiple project costings to prioritise capex). Re-opening the CFO via Rogeliza's warm intro is the ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process the buyer is running is a procurement-led S2P evaluation that has been repeatedly interrupted by leadership change and capex prioritisation rather than run to a clean RFP-to-award timeline. Procurement (Jerry Cabiles VP, Rogeliza Rana Head Of Sourcing, Joseph Ortega PAA Head) drives internally; workshops (functional/technical/commercial) were run in Aug 2024, IT completed a subsystem assessment, and a GCash win created a halo. The current state (May 2026) is explicitly 'reboot mode' after multiple management changes, with Joseph planning to present Zycus to management before a planned F2F with Anton, Jerry, Rogeliza and Joseph. The approval chain runs through the CFO and new C-level team, but the sequence from shortlist to formal RFP to award has not been locked.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are known in outline but not fully documented in an active RFP. The evaluation is a full S2P suite fit (vendor risk, sourcing, contracts, procurement, invoicing) against the SAP Ariba baseline, with the earlier RFI confirmed on 2 May 2024 and Zycus requirements cited as the basis for the revised RFP (17 Mar). AI capability is an explicit weighted criterion: formal buyer-side AI clarifications were requested (21 Jul 2025 'Add info on AI requested', 1 Aug 2025 'AI clarification responded') and a Chief AI Officer audience was arranged. Integration/subsystem compatibility with Ariba-linked systems (IT Tech Implication Assessment completed 25 Oct 2024, Maximo rationalisation) is a hard technical criterion. No Avoma calls exist to confirm scoring weightings, so the criteria remain inferred from Next Step and activity history.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. It's genuinely competitive and could go either way; the reads below say where it stands.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Thin signals pull the read to 47.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) is a strong, active champion who indicated Zycus was leading in 2024 and brokered a CFO intro; Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) has developed into a second active advocate presenting Zycus internally as Ariba renewals near. Both are procurement-side with no budget authority, so the sponsorship is not yet at the executive level needed to close.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 32 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found; Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 47 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); exec access strength +0.30 (weight 15). Offsetting it: competitive strength -1.00 (weight 15).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 2 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 23 is anchored on the stage win (47), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 32. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

reasons.detail: GLOBE TELECOM is a $350k forecasted Best-Case opportunity at Shortlisted, but shows zero logged activity, movements, or calls in the 14-day window and the last touch was 18 days ago. The next step still sits on a 6 Jun plan to lock a Manila F2F with Jerry, Rogeliza, Joseph and Anton that has not advanced, while both champions (Rogeliza, Joseph) lack budget authority and the CFO (Rizza Eala) has had no engagement in ~23 months. If the silence holds, the approaching SAP Ariba renewal becomes a low-friction do-nothing and we lose the wedge before the 2026 evaluation.

Budget is unconfirmed for 2026 with group capex under review, and the deal value was cut sharply in June, so price justification and a budget owner are still missing.
The 21 May AI walkthrough with Chief AI Officer Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio kept the board-level AI sponsor engaged but did not advance the procurement award.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 30 Apr 2024, 794 days old. Stage Shortlisted; no submission-date fields populated so time-in-stage is not computable from the series. Close 31 Dec 2026 is 181 days out. RFP has been 'imminent' since Sep 2024 and was never issued; close date has slipped five times (2025-08-15 to 2025-10-15 to 2025-12-15 to 2026-01-15 to 2026-07-15 to 2026-12-31). To close by year-end still requires RFP issuance, multi-vendor evaluation, commercials, EB/CFO sign-off, InfoSec, ERP integration scoping and legal execution in under 26 weeks against an entrenched incumbent, which is at least 8 weeks behind the pace needed. Realistic close is H1 2027.
evidence: Last activity 15 Jun 2026 and inbound buyer emails through 2 Jun 2026 (Joseph Ortega asking about the Supply Chain Risk Management module) keep the deal inside the live window, but recent rep touches are largely event invites logged as 'Call Attempted'.

No formal RFP ever issued: Next Step history flags RFP 'imminent/due' across end-Sep 2024, end-Oct 2024 and Jan 2025, then reboot mode by May 2026; most recent RFP mention is 17 Mar (Rog expects revised RFP soon, Zycus requirements are the basis).
Economic Buyer not engaged since 31 Jul 2024, when Carl met CEO Ernest, CFO Rizza Eala, Finance Controller Walter Ricarte and Jerry Cabiles; the 6 Jun 2026 plan targets procurement, not the CFO.
Deal value halved from 650,000 to 350,000 on 6 Jun 2026 (Carl Kimball), a negative commercial signal alongside the 16 Mar 2026 close-date push to 31 Dec 2026.
SAP Ariba is the incumbent with renewals approaching (contract extended 3 months per 22 Aug 2024); the primary risk is a do-nothing renewal rather than a switch.

Repeated stalls: leadership turnover (new CEO/CFO/CIO settled 22 Aug 2024), group-wide capex prioritisation (19 Jun 2024) and Maximo subsystem rationalisation (25 Nov 2024) have each delayed the award.
Champions Rogeliza Rana (Head Of Sourcing) and Joseph Ortega (PAA Head) are active but neither controls budget; without CFO sponsorship they cannot close.
Forecast is Best Case but lacks an engaged EB, a confirmed 2026 budget and locked commercials, so the category is indefensible.
north_star_verdict.headline: Live but slow: procurement contacts (Joseph Ortega, Rogeliza) are engaged and Zycus is being presented internally as SAP Ariba renewals near, but the formal RFP has slipped repeatedly for two years, the economic buyer (CFO) has not been engaged since Jul 2024, and the deal value was cut from 650K to 350K on 6 Jun 2026. Close by 31 Dec 2026 is not credible on current pace.

flags: Re-open the economic buyer: use Rogeliza's prior warm intro to get CFO Rizza Eala engaged before the SAP Ariba renewal locks the status quo.
Neutralise the incumbent: build a quantified switching-cost and Ariba-renewal-avoidance case so the buyer is not defaulting to a do-nothing renewal.
Activate the AI wedge: put Merlin agentic AI in front of Chief AI Officer Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio to hold the differentiation Ariba and Coupa cannot match.
best_practice_check.summary: Win levers for a MID-stage, incumbent-defended S2P deal that has stalled on formal process and executive sponsorship.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba (incumbent) is the single strongest current threat: contract extended 3 months and renewals approaching per 6 Jun 2026, so the dominant risk is a do-nothing renewal, not a rival platform. Coupa ran a workshop before Zycus (31 Jul 2024) but shows no recent buyer lean and ranks below the incumbent. iValua is named only in the field with no corroborating evidence and is dormant. Zycus was indicated to be leading in 2024 (Rogeliza, 16 Sep 2024) with the AI/Merlin story and the GCash reference as the wedge.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Globe expects a full S2P suite that can displace SAP Ariba, integrate cleanly with Ariba-linked subsystems, and deliver a credible AI/agentic transformation story to a board-level AI sponsor. Zycus fits on suite breadth and AI narrative; the open exposure is a quantified business case and integration proof.

hard.ais_why:
• Per the 27 Oct rep note: 'Meeting the Ayala AI Chief AI Office, to see how we can propose an AI driven transformation strategy Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio (currently at Globe, and also is on the board at GCash- 12/13/14 in Manila 2nd degree connection2nd 7th Oct Good introduction meeting planned for Chief AI Officer (from Globe, but runs Ayala Conglom AI strategy) to demonstrate Zycus AI week of 20th- Carl/Akshay.' Anton Reynaldo Bonifacio, Chief AI Officer at Globe (who also runs Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy), is explicitly named as the AI-driven transformation strategy contact.
• Per the 1 Aug 2025 entry: 'AI clarification responded.' Per 21 Jul 2025: 'Add info on AI requested.' These are formal buyer-side AI-clarification requests within the tender process. Per the 13 Feb entry: 'Globe exec's part of GCash signing ceremony. RFP to be issued in 1 week.' GCash (Globe subsidiary, already a Zycus win via LOI) created a halo. Per 16 Mar 2026: 'Sent intro mail and meeting request.'
• Confidence is Medium. Globe has a named Chief AI Officer who is also Ayala Conglomerate's AI strategy lead ‚Äî a senior AI sponsor. Product scope contains no AI SKUs (iContract; iSource; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iRequest) but the captured dialogue includes buyer-originated AI clarifications and a Chief-AI-Officer audience. Caveats: BD_Engagement is Unengaged, multiple management changes have stalled the deal, and current state is reboot mode (May 2026). Score 7 reflects board-level AI sponsorship despite organisational disruption.
